meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell with this SaaS starter kit guide. Master rapid development using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate fatigue and focus on core revenue-generating features. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you move from zero to a deployed, production-ready MVP in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'To-Do List' tutorials while your real product idea gathers dust. Every hour spent following a generic CRUD tutorial is an hour you aren't talking to customers or validating your pricing model. The friction isn't your coding ability; it's the lack of a standardized foundation that handles the mundane complexities of modern SaaS architecture.

True velocity comes from modularity and intelligent automation. By adopting a 'Starter Kit' mindset—where authentication, payment integration, and database schema are pre-baked—you reclaim your focus. We are shifting from manual coding to 'AI-assisted architecting,' where you define the business logic and let high-performance tools generate the heavy lifting.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish your tech stack and database schema before writing a single line of code. This ensures your MVP is scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-performance SaaS architecture blueprint for a B2B productivity app. Include Supabase for auth/DB, Stripe for billing, and Next.js for the frontend. Focus on modular structure.

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the core boilerplate, ensuring your project follows industry best practices for security and performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the directory structure for a production-grade SaaS. Include folders for services, hooks, components, and middleware. Ensure type safety with TypeScript.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Use your starter kit's pre-built integration to connect your app to Stripe webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler in Next.js that updates the user's subscription status in the Supabase 'profiles' table upon successful payment.

steps.title: Build Core Business Logic
steps.description: Now that the plumbing is done, use Claude Code to rapidly build your unique value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a functional component for the user dashboard that calculates and displays real-time analytics based on the 'events' table in Supabase.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline to get your MVP in front of real users immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CI/CD configuration file for Vercel deployment that includes automated linting and unit testing checks.

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product, not just a repo; update it every time you solve a new recurring problem.
Always use Environment Variables for API keys; never hardcode secrets, even in 'private' prototypes.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature parity; cut every non-essential feature that doesn't directly contribute to checkout.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your Auth middleware; it is the most common point of failure for new SaaS founders.
Implement feature flags early so you can toggle experimental features without rolling back your entire production build.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, push your code to production, set up your analytics tracking, and spend 80% of your time on customer acquisition rather than feature development.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building toy apps and start building a business. Don't let perfectionism kill your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
